note:
在我们开始像一群PlayStation滑板迷那样拥抱彼此，尖叫着经典的流行朋克歌曲，称赞本周新推出的托尼·霍克(Tony Hawk)的职业滑冰选手1+2之前，让我们记住上一次怀旧的人假扮超人是什么时候。
托尼·霍克(Tony Hawk)的专业滑冰高清游戏于2012年推出，是Xbox360和PS3一代的最后一次喘息，但它远没有像粉丝们希望的那样恢复滑板的辉煌--特别是在该系列在冲淡续集和外围附加功能后被注销的情况下。作为前两款超级流行的托尼·霍克(Tony Hawk)游戏的汇编，THPSHD只收录了这两款游戏加起来的7个滑冰场。从技术上讲，它的高清状态可能是真实的，但不可靠的物理、可疑的色彩映射以及损坏的大落差系统使其远未确定。
八年后，动视(Activision)的这一系列操纵者们又回来了，推出了前两款托尼·霍克(Tony Hawk)游戏，再次处于新一代游戏机的风口浪尖。好消息是，这一次，托尼·霍克的职业滑冰选手1+2是…。好的。就像，它真的很棒。
推销很简单：该系列前两款PlayStation游戏中的每一个滑板公园都回来了，都点缀着现代图形的繁华，而在其他方面则类似于原始的几何图形。同样，游戏也像传统的那样崩溃了--混合了经典的战役、充足的免费溜冰选项、多人对决和大规模的创建公园模式。
在过去的一天里，我花了很多时间玩了很多翻拍的PC版游戏，我可以报告说，从头开始重建的控制和物理(大部分)都是对的--所有主要的不同之处都取决于从原始游戏的刷新率上的跃升。曾经以低于30fps的速度运行的东西现在可以在任意分辨率下以解锁的速度运行，我非常享受我144 Hz监视器上的效果。
机械地说，将托尼·霍克拼凑在一起的多工作室团队似乎在忠于前两款PlayStation游戏方面犯了错误。你跳得有多高；你在半空中盘旋多长时间，同时举起半根管子；你拉把戏时身体旋转的速度有多快；需要多长时间才能制作动画：给现代版本及其PS1的前辈设置秒表，它们几乎是1：1。这与前两款游戏的Xbox和Dreamcast版本略有不同，这两款游戏都增加了上述大多数指标的浮动性。
如果你最美好的THPS记忆来自90年代，那么你很可能会记得游戏是在CRT面板上玩的，这种屏幕技术得益于按钮点击和屏幕操作之间的较低延迟。当我重新玩THPS1+2时，当我在技巧计时上挣扎时，我首先想到的是：我的显示器是我高分和病态技巧的瓶颈吗？是我的屏幕太烂了，还是只有我这么想？因此，我抓起我的三星Galaxy S9，切换到超慢动作视频，然后开始工作。
在默认的显示器和GPU设置下，我的PC版游戏显示了大约16帧画面，从轻按按钮(使用Xbox One游戏板，通过2.4 GHz无线适配器连接)到重要的游戏内动画开始(在我的测试中，在拉奥利跳上滑板之前，膝盖轻微弯曲)之间出现了大约16帧画面。在我的测试中，这款游戏从点击按钮(使用Xbox One游戏板，通过2.4 GHz无线适配器连接)到重要的游戏动画开始之间，显示了大约16帧画面。在144 Hz的监视器上，这16帧大约相当于0.11秒。不过，根据您的GPU制造商的不同，您可以切换不同类型的低延迟模式。在我的例子中，我进入了NVIDIA控制面板，将&#34；低延迟模式切换到&#34；(不是&#34；Ultra&34；)，然后再试一次。这将点击到动画的时间减少了一半，从16帧减少到8帧(减少到0.05秒)。在做了这个改变之后，我确实开始做得更好了。(同样，这只是滑冰者实际跳跃之前的动画。毫秒很重要。)
如果没有PS4或Xbox One上的游戏副本可供比较，我只会估计那些连接到标准高清电视并以60fps速度运行的系统的输入延迟计数比连接到高刷新率显示器的高端PC更容易漂浮。大多数情况下，我不得不接受当你的角色的动画运行得更流畅时，系列经典的控制和计时会有什么不同的感觉，而你的里程将相当诚实地取决于开关对你的打击。(失效的THPS玩家应该会期待短暂的挫折感，因为他们会习惯它。)。
如果这看起来有点矫枉过正，那么你显然从来没有尝试过在经典的托尼·霍克游戏中积累精确的、由多个部分组成的滑冰技巧组合。至少在好消息上，本周的翻拍电影对评分系统进行了温和的改变，说明了现代银幕的现实。如果你错过了你的花招落地角度，落地窗口已经加宽，只有草率落地会被扣分(或者完美落地会获得分数奖励)。然而，这一调整并不适用于从半空踢脚到铁轨打磨时流血的特技动画。(尤其是这些东西一直在踢我的屁股。)。
考虑到所有这些，这些控制装置可以最好地描述为托尼·霍克(Tony Hawk 2-Plus)。每一个特技链条都可以用手册来扩展(弹出滑板，同时保持你的双脚平衡)和恢复(从半管滑板上光滑地旋转)，而从后来的续集中抬起的最大的机械师是墙体植物，它可以让你用圆滑的旋转从墙上弹起。(从半根管子上滑转着落地)，而从后来的续集中抬起的最大的机械师是墙体植物，它可以让你用滑板反弹离开墙壁。(旋转光滑的半管着地)，而从后来的续集中抬起的最大的机械师是墙体植物，它让你用滑板弹离墙壁。否则，机械系统大多是老式的。速度是由前两场比赛中相同的特殊米控制的，而大落差的机械师已经被移除，所以只要你的滑板与地面对齐，你就可以在疯狂跌倒中幸存下来。
如果您想跳到您最喜欢的旧水平，并滑冰到您的心的内容，每一个标准的课程是立即解锁的，以此为目的。(这意味着，THPS1+2通过了Ars Technica的游戏翻拍指标。)。这场经典的活动基本上是对最初的两款游戏在每门课程上的目标进行了1：1的翻新，尽管旧游戏的提现已经变成了一种更简单的升级点货币。在大多数情况下，您可以观看一个经典的PS1演练，找到所有这个游戏的最好的技术和收藏指南。
这背后是一个全面的新成就系统，这很容易就是游戏中最好的底层更新。从简单的目标开始，比如建造你的第一个公园球场，或者和一位著名的滑板运动员(比如托尼·霍克900)一起完成一个特定的技巧。从那时起，逐步实现更疯狂的目标，比如将特定的技巧组合在一起。这个完全可选的系统可以让你积累游戏中的现金来为你的角色购买服装、配件和滑板，在发布时没有明显的真实货币系统。动视历来都会在游戏上线后偷偷将真货偷偷带进游戏中，但近年来，他们对这种做法有所缓和，所以我们希望托尼·霍克的粉丝在购买了这款悬而未决的游戏后，可以把信用卡收起来。
我已经开始欣赏这些经典的滑板公园所用的油漆，尽管我真的希望它们能有更多动态的照明选择。每个滑冰公园都沐浴在漂亮的灯光效果中，根据一天中被石头锁住的时间，所有这些效果都针对逼真的对比度和阴影进行了优化。问题是，它在明显的阴影中覆盖了一些经典的水平伸展，这玩弄了我在尝试一系列技巧时所寻找的肌肉记忆。
对于任何记得威尼斯海滨滑冰公园的人来说，这更像是一种挑剔，比如，威尼斯海滨滑冰公园是一个明亮、灯光均匀的区域，有跳跃、研磨和战略性安排的VBGAP半管道。我个人喜欢看到这些重新设计的公园中的一些以不同的时间效果点亮，但开发者选择了预烘焙的灯光效果来保持高帧率运行。(公平交易。)。每一个新渲染的课程在导航和技巧执行方面都是清晰和可读的，所以你不应该担心把这个交给你家族中的一个全新的托尼·霍克(Tony Hawk)球员。
由于他们忠于老式游戏机的限制，THPS1+2；的课程在玩了几个小时后开始感到有点幽闭恐惧症。的确，每个课程中曲折的小径、隐蔽的房间和宽敞的广场的数量提醒人们，在90年代末，Neversoft在PS1硬件上挤出了多少空间，在2020年，你将会在这些精心安排的课程中获得很多乐趣。(另外，后来Tony Hawk游戏特别因为超大、臃肿的c
因为这只是一种印象，我只能报告在线多人模式的功能大多如广告所说的那样，而不是提供深度潜水。各种经典的游戏模式，从得分攻击到涂鸦等古怪模式，在公开的在线相亲大堂里轮流对抗多达7名其他玩家，在这些模式下，玩家之间不会发生碰撞，所以至少他们没有被骗子毁掉(尽管我已经看到过在线玩家获得看似不可能的分数，完全不知道他们是伟大的玩家还是伟大的黑客)。奇怪的是，虽然朋友们可以在同一张沙发上设置本地的分屏模式，即使是在PC上(谢天谢地)，但他们不能为仅限朋友参加的滑冰挑战创建定制的在线大堂。我现在不能邀请朋友来我家(嗨，大流行)，我真的很想和朋友们分享这个经典的游戏美好，而不是沉默的陌生人，Activision。
剩下的体验很容易总结：Create-a-Park模式充满了很酷的选项，还有上传和分享你的创作的简单方式(并下载由主要工作室Vicarious Visions制作的免费)；几乎每一首获得许可的摇滚、流行朋克和嘻哈歌曲都会从前两款游戏中返回，连同合身的新歌，总共有60首曲目；你可以从第一届游戏中的每一位现实世界的滑板运动员中挑选，还有后起之秀和四名Create-a-a滑板选手。
结论：动视没有把这件事搞砸。如果你想回到老式的，精干而中庸的托尼·霍克的职业滑冰运动员，那就买吧。
note_en:
Before we begin putting arms around each other as a bunch of PlayStation skateboarding fans, screaming classic pop-punk songs and praising this week's new  Tony Hawk's Pro Skater 1+2, let's remember the last time a nostalgic cash-in pretended to be a Superman.
 Tony Hawk's Pro Skater HD launched in 2012 as a last gasp of the Xbox 360 and PS3 generation, but it was far from the return to skateboarding glory that fans had hoped for—especially as the series had been written off after watered-down sequels and peripheral add-ons. As a "compilation" of the first two mega-popular Tony Hawk games,  THPSHD only included seven skateparks from both games combined. Its "HD" status may have technically been true, but glitchy physics, questionable color-mapping, and a broken "big drop" system made it far from definitive.
 Eight years later, the series' handlers at Activision are back with another crack at the first two Tony Hawk games, once again at the cusp of a new console generation. In good news, this time,  Tony Hawk's Pro Skater 1+2 is... good. Like, it's really good.
     The sales pitch is simple: Every skatepark from the series' first two PlayStation games is back, all touched up with modern graphical flourishes while otherwise resembling the courses' original geometry. In kind, gameplay breaks down like it traditionally did—a mix of a "classic" campaign, ample "free skate" options, multiplayer versus, and a massive create-a-park mode.
 Having spent the past day playing a lot of the remake's PC version, I can report that the rebuilt-from-scratch controls and physics are (mostly) on point—with every major difference hinging on a jump from the original game's refresh rate. What once natively ran at under 30fps can now run at an unlocked rate at arbitrary resolutions, and I've seriously enjoyed the results on my 144Hz monitor.
 Mechanically, the multi-studio team that pieced Tony Hawk back together appears to have erred on the side of allegiance to the first two PlayStation games. How high you jump; how long you hover in mid-air while boosting up a half-pipe; how quickly your body rotates as you pull a trick; how long it takes tricks to animate: Set a stopwatch on the modern versions and their PS1 forebears, and they're pretty much 1:1. This differs slightly from the Xbox and Dreamcast versions of the first two games, which each added floatiness to most of the above metrics.
 If your brightest  THPS memories come from the '90s, then you're likely to remember the game as played on a CRT panel, a screen technology that benefits from lower latency between button taps and on-screen action. This was the first thing I thought about when I struggled with trick timing while playing  THPS1+2 anew: Is my monitor the bottleneck for my high scores and sick tricks? Does my screen suck, or is it just me? So I grabbed my Samsung Galaxy S9, toggled super slow-motion video, and got down to business.
  On default monitor and GPU settings, my PC version of the game shows roughly 16 frames between a button tap (using an Xbox One gamepad, connected via 2.4GHz wireless adapter) and a significant in-game animation beginning (in my test's case, a mild bending of the knees before pulling an "ollie" hop on a skateboard). On a 144Hz monitor, those 16 frames equate to roughly 0.11 seconds. Depending on your GPU manufacturer, however, you can toggle different types of "low latency" modes. In my case, I went into an Nvidia Control Panel, switched "low latency" mode to "on" (not "ultra") and tried again. This cut the tap-to-animation time in half, from 16 frames to 8 frames (down to 0.05 seconds). After making this switch, I did indeed start doing better. (Again, this is just the animation before your skater actually hops. Milliseconds matter.)
 Without a copy of the game on either PS4 or Xbox One to compare, I'll merely estimate that those systems' input latency counts, as connected to standard HDTVs and running at 60fps, are floatier than a high-end PC connected to a high refresh-rate monitor. Mostly, I've had to come to terms with how the series' classic controls and timings feel different when your character's animations run that much more smoothly, and your mileage will quite honestly hinge on how that switch strikes you. (Lapsed  THPS players should expect short-lived frustrations getting used to it.)
     If that seems like overkill, you've clearly never tried racking up precise, multi-part skate-trick combos in a classic Tony Hawk game before. In great news, at least, this week's remake accounts for the realities of modern screens with a mild change to the scoring system. Should you miss your trick's landing angle, the window for landing has been widened, only with a score penalty for "sloppy" landings (or enjoy a score bonus for a "perfect" landing). However, that tweak doesn't apply to trick animations that bleed from, say, a mid-air kickflip to a rail grind. (These in particular have been kicking my butt.)
 With all of that in mind, the controls can best be described as " Tony Hawk 2-Plus." Every trick chain can be extended with "manuals" (pop a skateboard "wheelie" while keeping your feet balanced) and "reverts" (land from a half-pipe with a slick rotation), while the biggest mechanic lifted from a later sequel is the wall plant, which lets you bounce off a wall with your foot instead of having a trick chain end with a wall collision. Otherwise, the mechanical system is mostly old school. Speed is managed with the same "special" meter from the first two games, while the "big drop" mechanic has been removed, so you can survive crazy falls so long as your skateboard lines up with the ground.
 Should you wish to jump into your favorite old level and skate around to your heart's content, every standard course is unlocked immediately for this purpose. (Meaning,  THPS1+2 passes  Ars Technica's metric for game remakes.) The "classic" campaign is mostly a 1:1 retread of the original two games' collect-a-thon goals on each course, though the old games' "cash" pickups have been turned into a simpler "upgrade point" currency. For the most part, you can watch a classic PS1 walkthrough to find all of this game's best techniques and collectible guides.
 Behind this is a comprehensive new "achievements" system, which is easily the game's best under-the-hood update. Start with simple goals like building your first create-a-park course or pulling off a specific trick with a famous skateboarder (like Tony Hawk's 900). From there, graduate to crazier goals like chaining together specific tricks in a combo. This entirely optional system lets you accrue in-game cash to buy outfits, accessories, and skateboards for your characters, with no apparent real-money system as of launch. Activision has a history of sneaking real purchases into games after launch, but in recent years, they've relented on this practice, so we're hopeful Tony Hawk fans can put their credit cards away after buying the danged game.
   I've grown to appreciate the wash of paint applied to these classic skateparks, though I do wish they'd come with more dynamic lighting options. Each skatepark is bathed in handsome lighting effects, all optimized for realistic levels of contrast and shadow depending on whatever time of day has been locked in stone. The trouble is, this covers some classic levels' stretches in noticeable shadows, and this has toyed with my muscle memory of what I'm looking for as I pilot a chain of tricks.
 That's more of a nitpick for anyone who remembers, say, the Venice beachside skatepark as a bright, evenly lit zone of hops, grinds, and strategically arranged "VB gap" half-pipes. I'd personally like to see some of these handsomely reimagined parks light up with different time-of-day effects, but the developers chose pre-baked lighting effects to keep things running at a high frame rate. (Fair trade.) Every newly rendered course is otherwise clear and readable in terms of navigation and trick execution, so you shouldn't worry about handing this to a brand-new Tony Hawk player in your family.
 Due to their allegiance to older console limitations,  THPS1+2's courses start to feel a bit claustrophobic after a few hours of play. Really, the number of twisting paths, hidden rooms, and spacious plazas in each course is a reminder of how much Neversoft squeezed out of PS1 hardware in the late '90s, and you'll have plenty of fun tearing through these smartly arranged courses in 2020. (Plus, later Tony Hawk games suffered specifically because of oversized, bloated courses.) But a little part of me wishes this year's dev team had found clever ways to sew one skatepark into the next, so that you could seamlessly smash through a window in the  THPS1 warehouse and land in the  THPS2 bullring. Alas.
 Because this is an impressions take, I can only report that the online multiplayer modes function mostly as advertised, as opposed to offering a deep dive. Various classic game modes, which range from score attacks to quirky modes like "graffiti," rotate in public online matchmaking lobbies against up to seven other players, and in these, players can't collide with each other, so at least they're not ruined by griefers (though I've already seen online players rack up seemingly impossible scores, without any sense of whether they're great players or great hackers). Weirdly, while friends can set up local split-screen modes on the same couch, even on PC (and thank goodness), they cannot create custom online lobbies for friends-only skate challenges. I currently cannot invite friends over to my home (hi, pandemic), and I'd really like to share this classic gaming goodness with friends, not silent strangers, Activision.
 The rest of the experience is easy to summarize: The create-a-park mode is full of cool options, along with easy ways to upload and share your creations (and download freebies made by lead studio Vicarious Visions); nearly every licensed rock, pop-punk, and hip-hop song returns from the first two games, along with fitting new ones, for a grand total of 60 tracks; and you can pick from every real-world skateboarder from the first games, along with up-and-coming skaters and four create-a-skater slots.
 Verdict: Activision didn't screw this up. If you're fiending for a return to old-school, lean-and-mean  Tony Hawk's Pro Skater, buy.
title: 托尼·霍克的职业滑冰选手1+2 PC印象：这个超人没有伪装
title_en: Tony Hawk’s Pro Skater 1+2 PC impressions: No pretending for this Superman

美国国防部周五重申，决定授予微软100亿美元的绝地军事云计算合同，在争夺这份利润丰厚的合同的法律战中，这对亚马逊是一个打击。
微软最初在去年10月赢得这份合同，但在亚马逊在诉讼中对这一决定提出质疑后，美国国防部在4月启动了一项审查，称采购过程中存在错误。
亚马逊声称，官员们没有正确评估提案的一些技术方面，五角大楼偏袒微软，因为唐纳德·特朗普总统个人不喜欢拥有《华盛顿邮报》的CEO杰夫·贝佐斯。
五角大楼在一份声明中说，它“已经完成了对绝地云提案的全面重新评估，并确定微软的提案对政府来说仍然是最有价值的。”
微软的一位发言人在一份声明中说，“我们感谢国防部经过仔细审查，确认我们提供了正确的技术和最佳的价值。”
亚马逊在一篇博客文章中说，国防部“基于政治和不正当影响，而不是基于两个产品的相对优势，授予了另一份合同”。
他说：“采取纠正行动本应为国防部提供一个机会，以解决我们的抗议中所概述的众多重大评估错误，确保公平和公平的竞争环境，并最终加快诉讼的结束。”不幸的是，国防部拒绝了这个机会，“帖子上写道。
甚至在亚马逊输掉比赛之前，绝地的合同就已经饱受争议。IBM和甲骨文(Oracle)也在竞标这份合同，它们在2019年初抱怨称，这一过程对亚马逊有偏见。作为对特朗普参与指控的回应，国防部监察长调查了来自白宫的任何不当影响，但没有发现任何不偏袒亚马逊的压力的证据。尽管4月份的报告说，白宫的关键证人被调查人员阻止接受讯问，导致监察长指出，它无法确定白宫和国防部之间任何互动的“全部性质”。
在亚马逊的法律挑战得到解决期间，一名法官禁止微软开始合同工作。亚马逊表示，将继续在法庭上抗辩这一决定，这可能会进一步推迟合同。
note_en:
The Department of Defense on Friday reaffirmed its decision to award Microsoft a $10 billion JEDI military cloud computing contract, dealing a blow to Amazon amid a legal fight for the lucrative contract.
      Microsoft originally  won the contract in October last year, but the DoD launched a review in April after Amazon challenged the decision in a lawsuit, alleging there were errors in the procurement process.
  Amazon  claimed that officials did not properly evaluate some technical aspects of the proposals, and that the Pentagon was biased in favor of Microsoft because of President Donald Trump’s personal dislike of CEO Jeff Bezos, who owns the  Washington Post.
  The Pentagon  said in a statement it “has completed its comprehensive re-evaluation of the JEDI Cloud proposals and determined that Microsoft's proposal continues to represent the best value to the Government.”
  A Microsoft spokesperson said in a statement, “We appreciate that after careful review, the DoD confirmed that we offered the right technology and the best value.”
    In a blog  post, Amazon said the DoD gave “another contract award based on politics and improper influence and not based on the relative strengths of the two offerings.”
    “Taking corrective action should have provided the DoD an opportunity to address the numerous material evaluation errors outlined in our protest, ensure a fair and level playing field, and ultimately, expedite the conclusion of litigation. Unfortunately, the DoD rejected that opportunity,” the post reads.
    The JEDI contract was steeped in controversy even before Amazon lost. IBM and Oracle, which were also up for the contract, complained in early 2019 the process was biased in favor of Amazon. In response to allegations about Trump’s involvement, the DoD inspector general investigated any improper influence from the White House, but found no evidence of any pressure to disfavor Amazon. Though the April  report says that key witnesses from the White House were blocked from being interviewed by investigators, leading the inspector general to note that it could not determine the “full nature of extent” of any interactions between the White House and DoD.
    Microsoft has been barred from starting work on the contract by a judge while Amazon’s legal challenge is resolved. Amazon said will continue to fight the decision in court, which will likely delay the contract even more.
title: 五角大楼将绝地合同授予微软-再次打击亚马逊
title_en: Pentagon Awards JEDI Contract To Microsoft—Again—In Blow To Amazon

联邦通信委员会(Federal Communications Commission)主席阿吉特·派(Ajit Pai)吹捧不准确的宽带可用性数据，以声称他的放松监管议程加快了部署，尽管有明确的警告迹象表明，FCC依赖虚假信息。
Pai在2019年2月声称，FCC基准速度下行25 Mbps，上行3 Mbps的固定宽带的美国人数从2016年底的2610万人下降到2017年底的1940万人，他将这一改善归功于FCC消除了基础设施投资的障碍。这些数字包括在FCC国会授权的年度宽带评估草案中，Pai要求其他委员批准得出宽带行业正在做的结论的报告。
但消费者权益倡导组织自由新闻(Free Press)随后指出，这些数字被一家名为BarrierFree的ISP扭曲了。该ISP突然声称，在拥有近6200万人口的人口普查区部署了光纤到户和固定无线服务(每项服务的下行/上行速度分别为940 Mbps/880 Mbps)。这是一个不可信的断言，意味着BarrierFree在短短6个月内就从服务于零人增加到近20%的美国人口。当时，当ARS联系到BarrierFree时，他承认了这个错误，称在上传过程中，提交的一部分内容被错误解析。
派更正了数据，承认2130万美国人仍然缺乏宽带，但并没有改变他的结论，即宽带正在以合理和及时的方式部署到所有美国人手中。(即使是这个2130万；数字也可能是低估的，因为联邦通信委员会将整个人口普查区算作服务，即使人口普查区只有一个家庭可以使用宽带服务。)。
本周，FCC公布了BarrierFree明显违反规定的更多细节，该规定要求互联网服务提供商每6个月提交一次宽带部署数据，报告显示，早在Pai吹捧不准确的数据之前，FCC工作人员就发现了大量警告信号。周二，FCC发布了一份表面责任通知，提议对BarrierFree处以16.3912美元的罚款，启动了一个程序，让BarrierFree有机会对指控做出回应，并反对拟议的处罚。
尽管FCC试图对BarrierFree提交不准确的数据处以罚款，但委员会并不是因为该ISP未能提交超过10年的所需表格477报告而对其进行惩罚。
Pai FCC睡在BarrierFree一再违反FCC规则上，自由新闻研究总监Derek Turner告诉Ars，称FCC试图淡化自己在散布不准确数据方面的作用是可耻的。
FCC在表面责任通知中表示，BarrierFree于2004年5月开始为纽约州萨福克县的客户提供服务，但十多年来没有提交任何所需的477表格申请，因为它后来告诉FCC，它认为这些申请是自愿的，除非ISP申请政府拨款。
FCC工作人员在2015年11月给BarrierFree发了一封电子邮件，称ISP最终提交477表格报告的尝试尚未完成，需要采取进一步行动。FCC工作人员在2016年1月再次联系BarrierFree，称对于有效且仍未提交的文件，请记住，如果您的公司不遵守规定，可能会被提交给执法局。
BarrierFree最终在2018年3月提交了一份完整的477表格申请，报告称尽管只为纽约一个地区的客户提供服务，但在东北部和大西洋中部各州广泛部署。这份文件在FCC工作人员中敲响了警钟，原因有几个，比如ISP声称，它在一个长岛人口普查区域的住宅宽带连接数量超过了该区域的实际家庭单元数量。
2018年6月5日，[FCC]工作人员通知BarrierFree您的文件中的某些项目不寻常，可能不准确，可能需要更正。2018年7月31日，工作人员重申了这一警告。
2018年7月11日，欧盟委员会工作人员发布了一份公开通知，提醒477名申请者，下一次提交的截止日期不晚于2018年9月4日。2018年8月29日，工作人员给BarrierFree发电子邮件说，提交截止日期即将到来。2018年9月18日，工作人员再次通知BarrierFree提交截止日期已过...。潜在的执法行动：请注意，委员会跟踪那些在截止日期后持续提交表格477的申请者。未能及时提交FCC表格477可能会导致罚款和处罚。2018年10月15日，工作人员重申了这一警告。
FCC表示，BarrierFree没有提交2018年9月所需的申请。FCC工作人员于2019年2月19日就即将到来的2019年3月最后期限再次向BarrierFree发出警告。然而，就在同一天，FCC主席办公室发布了一份新闻稿，兜售这份报告草案，其中包括因无障碍组织2018年3月提交的错误文件而夸大的部署数据。这份报告显示，我们的方法正在奏效，派在新闻稿中表示。
note_en:
Federal Communications Commission Chairman Ajit Pai touted inaccurate broadband-availability data in order to claim that his deregulatory agenda sped up deployment despite clear warning signs that the FCC was relying on false information.
 Pai  claimed in February 2019 that the number of Americans lacking access to fixed broadband at the FCC benchmark speed of 25Mbps downstream and 3Mbps upstream dropped from 26.1 million people at the end of 2016 to 19.4 million at the end of 2017, and he attributed the improvement to the FCC "removing barriers to infrastructure investment." The numbers were included in a draft version of the FCC's congressionally mandated annual broadband assessment, and Pai asked fellow commissioners to approve the report that concluded the broadband industry was doing enough to expand access.
 But consumer-advocacy group Free Press  subsequently pointed out that the numbers were skewed by an ISP called BarrierFree suddenly "claim[ing] deployment of fiber-to-the-home and fixed wireless services (each at downstream/upstream speeds of 940mbps/880mbps) to census blocks containing nearly 62 million persons." This is an implausible assertion and would have meant BarrierFree went from serving zero people to nearly 20 percent of the US population in just six months. BarrierFree admitted the error when contacted by Ars at the time, saying that "a portion of the submission was parsed incorrectly in the upload process."
 Pai  corrected the data to acknowledge that 21.3 million Americans still lacked broadband but didn't change his conclusion that broadband was "being deployed to all Americans in a reasonable and timely fashion." (Even that 21.3-million figure was  likely an undercount because the FCC  counts an entire census block as served even if only one home in the census block can get service.)
 This week, the FCC released more details on BarrierFree's apparent history of violating rules requiring ISPs to submit "Form 477" broadband-deployment data every six months, and it shows that numerous warning signs were spotted by FCC staff long before Pai touted the inaccurate data. The  FCC on Tuesday issued a Notice of Apparent Liability that proposed a $163,912 fine for BarrierFree, kicking off a process that gives BarrierFree a chance to respond to the allegations and fight the proposed penalty.
 Although the FCC is trying to fine BarrierFree for submitting inaccurate data, the commission is not penalizing the ISP for failing to submit over 10 years' worth of required Form 477 reports.
 "The Pai FCC slept on BarrierFree's repeated violation of FCC rules," Free Press Research Director Derek Turner told Ars, calling the FCC's attempt to downplay its own role in spreading inaccurate data "shameful."
  BarrierFree began serving customers in Suffolk County, New York, in May 2004 but did not submit any of the required Form 477 filings for more than a decade because, it later told the FCC, it thought the filings were voluntary unless an ISP was applying for government grants, the FCC said in the Notice of Apparent Liability.
 FCC staff emailed BarrierFree in November 2015, saying that the ISP's attempt to finally submit a Form 477 report was incomplete and "further action is needed." FCC staff contacted BarrierFree again in January 2016, saying that "For filings that are valid and remain un-submitted, please keep in mind that your company may be referred to the Enforcement Bureau for non-compliance."
 BarrierFree finally submitted a complete Form 477 filing in March 2018, reporting widespread deployment in Northeast and Mid-Atlantic states despite only serving customers in one part of New York. The filing set off alarm bells among FCC staff for several reasons, such as the ISP claiming that it had more residential broadband connections in one Long Island census tract than the number of actual household units in the tract.
  On June 5, 2018, [FCC] staff notified BarrierFree of "certain items in your filing which are unusual and potentially inaccurate, and corrections may be necessary." On July 31, 2018, staff repeated that admonition.
 On July 11, 2018, Commission staff issued a Public Notice reminding Form 477 filers that the next filing would be due no later than September 4, 2018. On August 29, 2018, staff emailed BarrierFree that there was a "FILING DUE DATE APPROACHING." On September 18, 2018, staff again informed BarrierFree that "FILING DUE DATE MISSED... POTENTIAL ENFORCEMENT ACTION: Please note that the Commission tracks filers who consistently file Form 477 after the deadline. Failure to file FCC Form 477 in a timely manner may result in fines and penalties." And on October 15, 2018, staff reiterated that admonition.
 BarrierFree did not make the required September 2018 filing, the FCC said. FCC staff sent another warning to BarrierFree on February 19, 2019 about the upcoming March 2019 deadline. Yet on the very same day, the  FCC chairman's office issued a press release touting the draft report that included deployment data inflated by BarrierFree's incorrect March 2018 filing. "This report shows that our approach is working," Pai said in the press release.
title: Ajit Pai吹捧虚假的宽带数据，尽管有明确的迹象表明它不准确
title_en: Ajit Pai touted false broadband data despite clear signs it wasn’t accurate

进入Palantir向公开市场进军的下一阶段，我非常好奇地想知道，在即将到来的首次亮相期间，该公司将如何磨练其S-1申报文件，让自己获得尽可能好的机会。
该交易所探索初创企业、市场和资金。你可以每天早上在Extra Crunch上阅读它，或者每周六获得Exchange时事通讯。
我们最终拿到了一份新的S-1/A文件，我们自己的丹尼·克莱顿很快就分析并涵盖了这份文件。他发现的是一系列修正案，这些修正案似乎增加了三名Palantir内部人士永远控制公司50%以上投票权的可能性，可能使其成为一家受控公司，从而使公司不受选定的监管要求的约束。
丹尼冷淡地指出，“鉴于员工和投资者股票的投票权减弱，这些投票条款可能会对这些股票的最终价格产生负面影响。”这就是礼貌。
今天上午，我仔细考虑了这个问题，一直在想Snap和Facebook，前者在首次公开募股(IPO)中出售股票，新股东根本没有投票权，后者由马克·扎克伯格(Mark Zuckerberg)控制，作为他的私人领地。在这件事上，这两个人并不孤单。还有其他一些上市科技公司在每股基础上为特定的IPO前股东群体提供了比其他群体更多的投票权，尽管程度可能比Facebook管理的要小。
感觉很多初创公司(和以前的初创公司)随着时间的推移已经决定，让股东投入大量资金不是一个好主意。实际上，他们不仅要把公司当做君主制国家来经营，还要把它们当做无可置疑的君主制国家来经营。
我并不完全相信这是创造长期股东财富的最佳方式。
如果你在这道围栏的另一边，我理解。毕竟，Facebook是一家全球巨头，Snap最终勉强维持了股市涨幅，将其价值带回了上市时的水平。(为期三年的旅程。)。
但这些论据的说服力有限。你可以很容易地辩称，这两家公司本可以用更少的自我破坏(Facebook)和更多的支出纪律(Snap)做更多的事情。
note_en:
Heading into the next phase of Palantir’s march to the public markets, I was very curious to see how the company would hone its S-1 filing to give itself the best possible shot during its impending debut.
  The Exchange explores startups, markets and money. You can read it  every morning on Extra Crunch, or get  The Exchange newsletter every Saturday.
  And we finally did get a  new S-1/A filing, a document that our  own Danny Crichton quickly parsed and covered. What he found was a set of amendments that seem to increase the chance that three Palantir insiders will control more than 50% of the company’s voting power forever, possibly making it a controlled company which would loose the firm from select regulatory requirements.
 Danny dryly noted that “given the diminished voting power of employee and investor shares, it is possible that these voting provisions will negatively impact the final price of those shares.” That’s being polite.
 Mulling this over this morning, I kept thinking about  Snap,   which sold stock in its IPO that gave new shareholders  no votes at all, and Facebook, which is  controlled by Mark Zuckerberg  as his personal fiefdom. The two are not alone in this matter. There are a number of other public tech companies that provide certain groups of pre-IPO shareholders more votes than others on a per-share basis, though perhaps to a smaller degree than what Facebook has managed.
 It feels like many startups (and former startups) have decided over time that having material shareholder input is a bad idea. That, in effect, they must run companies as not merely monarchies, but unquestioned ones to boot.
 I am not entirely convinced that this is the best way to create long-term shareholder wealth.
 If you are on the other side of this particular fence, I understand. After all,  Facebook   is a global juggernaut and Snap has finally managed to eke out stock-market gains to bring its value it back where it was around when it went public. (A three-year journey.)
 But those arguments are only so good. You could easily argue that the two companies could have done much more with less self-sabotage (Facebook) and a bit more spend discipline (Snap).
title: Palantir的集中治理对高管来说是件好事，但股东呢？
title_en: Palantir’s concentrated governance is great for execs, but what about shareholders?

note:
五角大楼周五表示，它将坚持与微软就一项在法庭上争议数月的重要云合同进行谈判。
绝地，即联合企业防御基础设施，已经成为国防部竞争最激烈的合同之一。这份合同旨在使五角大楼庞大的IT基础设施现代化，价值可能高达100亿美元，用于在长达10年的时间内提供服务。
五角大楼在一份声明中说，国防部已经完成了对绝地云提案的全面重新评估，并确定微软的提案对政府来说仍然是最有价值的。绝地云合同是一份固定价格、不确定交付/不确定数量的合同，它将为国防部提供全方位的云计算服务。虽然由于联邦索赔法院于2020年2月13日发布的初步禁令，合同履行不会立即开始，但国防部渴望开始向我们穿制服的男女提供这一能力。
这一结果对亚马逊来说是一个损失，在五角大楼去年10月将合同授予微软后，亚马逊对合同的授予提出了挑战。
亚马逊在周五发表的一篇措辞严厉的博客文章中表示，它将继续寻求对情况进行评估，五角大楼对这些公司提议的重新评估只是验证了最初与微软合作的决定。
亚马逊写道，据报道，在绝地武士问题上，特朗普总统命令前国务卿马蒂斯去他的亚马逊，公然干预一项积极的采购，在宣布合同授予之前指示他的下属进行一次非正统的审查，然后阻碍了对他自己政治干预的调查。
虽然纠正措施可以用来有效地解决抗议活动，但在现实中，这种纠正行动什么也改变不了，浪费了本可以花五个月来弄清这些严重关切的时间，而且完全是为了分散我们对更广泛关切的注意力，重申一个被总统自身利益破坏的决定。
消息公布后，微软股价在周五的交易时段短暂走高，随后收盘下跌1.4%，科技股当天大多下跌。
我们感谢国防部在经过仔细审查后，确认我们提供了正确的技术和最佳的价值。微软的一位发言人在一封电子邮件中告诉CNBC，我们已经准备好开始工作，并确保那些为我们国家服务的人能够获得这项亟需的技术。
去年11月，亚马逊的云计算部门亚马逊网络服务公司(Amazon Web Services)向联邦索赔法院(Court Of Federal Claims)提起诉讼，抗议绝地武士的决定。该公司辩称，美国总统唐纳德·特朗普(Donald Trump)对亚马逊及其首席执行官杰夫·贝佐斯(Jeff Bezos)的偏见影响了五角大楼将合同交给微软。在这场法律战中，五角大楼邀请亚马逊和微软修改并重新提交合同提案。在五角大楼发表声明后，无法立即联系到司法部代表置评。
这份利润丰厚的合同原定于2018年9月授予，在特朗普去年表示他正在认真考虑研究绝地合同后，这份合同引起了密切关注。
2019年7月，特朗普在白宫告诉记者，我从来没有遇到过有更多人抱怨的事情。他补充说，世界上一些最伟大的公司都在抱怨这一点，并点名IBM、微软和甲骨文。白宫拒绝置评。
特朗普对这笔交易的潜在影响成为人们关注的焦点，当时一本书记录了时任国防部长詹姆斯·马蒂斯(James Mattis)在五角大楼的任期，书中称总统告诉马蒂斯将亚马逊从合同中剔除。
这位拥有华盛顿邮报(Washington Post)的亿万富翁高管已经成为特朗普的挫败源。总统经常批评该出版物对他的政府的报道。特朗普还多次抨击亚马逊，因为他声称，亚马逊没有缴纳公平份额的税款，并敲诈了美国邮政总局(U.S.Post Office)。
今年早些时候，五角大楼监察长发布了一份报告，称该奖项似乎没有受到白宫的影响。
然而，监察长在其313页的4月份报告中指出，在整个审查过程中，它与白宫官员的合作有限，因此无法完成对道德不当指控的评估。
当时，国防部发言人罗伯特·卡弗中校在一份声明中告诉CNBC，监察长的报告证实五角大楼的绝地云端采购过程是公平和依法进行的。
AWS的一位发言人当时表示，这份报告没有透露太多信息。
这位发言人在写给CNBC的一封电子邮件中写道，很明显，这份报告无法评估政治干预，因为白宫指示国防部的几名证人不要回答IG关于白宫和国防部官员之间沟通的问题。白宫拒绝配合IG的调查是又一次明目张胆地试图避免对绝地合同授予进行有意义和透明的审查。
去年12月，亚马逊AWS首席执行官安迪·贾西(Andy Jassy)告诉CNBC，云合同的判决不公平。
你知道，这里有严重的政治干预，贾西在与CNBC的乔恩·福特(Jon Fortt)交谈时谈到绝地奖时说。当你有一位在任总统愿意非常直言不讳地表示他们不喜欢一家公司和该公司的首席执行官时，包括国防部在内的政府机构很难在不担心报复的情况下做出客观的决定。我认为这对我们的国家来说是危险和危险的。
note_en:
The Pentagon said Friday it will stick with  Microsoft for a major cloud contract that has been disputed in court for months.
 The JEDI, or Joint Enterprise Defense Infrastructure, deal has become one of the most hotly contested contracts for the Department of Defense. The contract is intended to modernize the Pentagon's colossal IT infrastructure and could be valued up to $10 billion for services rendered over as many as 10 years.
  "The Department has completed its comprehensive re-evaluation of the JEDI Cloud proposals and determined that Microsoft's proposal continues to represent the best value to the Government," the Pentagon said in a statement. "The JEDI Cloud contract is a firm-fixed-price, indefinite-delivery/indefinite-quantity contract that will make a full range of cloud computing services available to the DoD. While contract performance will not begin immediately due to the Preliminary Injunction Order issued by the Court of Federal Claims on February 13, 2020, DoD is eager to begin delivering this capability to our men and women in uniform."
 The outcome represents a loss for  Amazon, which challenge the award of the contract after the Pentagon  gave it to Microsoft in October.
 Amazon said in a scathing  blog post Friday that it will continue to seek a review of the situation and that the Pentagon's re-evaluation of the companies' proposals simply validated the original decision to go with Microsoft.
 "On JEDI, President Trump reportedly ordered former Secretary Mattis to 'screw' Amazon, blatantly interfered in an active procurement, directed his subordinate to conduct an unorthodox 'review' prior to a contract award announcement and then stonewalled an investigation into his own political interference," Amazon wrote.
 "While corrective action can be used to efficiently resolve protests, in reality, this corrective action changed nothing, wasted five months that could have been spent getting to the bottom of these serious concerns, and was designed solely to distract from our broader concerns and reaffirm a decision that was corrupted by the President's self-interest."
  Microsoft shares briefly moved higher during Friday's trading session following the announcement before closing down 1.4% for the day in a mostly down day for tech stocks.
 "We appreciate that after careful review, the DoD confirmed that we offered the right technology and the best value. We're ready to get to work and make sure that those who serve our country have access to this much needed technology," a Microsoft spokesperson told CNBC in an email.
 In November, Amazon Web Services, Amazon's cloud computing unit,  filed a lawsuit in the Court of Federal Claims protesting the JEDI decision. The company argued that  President Donald Trump's bias against Amazon and its CEO, Jeff Bezos, influenced the Pentagon to give the contract to Microsoft. Amid the legal battle, the Pentagon invited Amazon and Microsoft to revise and resubmit their proposals for the contract. A Justice Department representative was not immediately available for comment following the Pentagon's statement.
  The lucrative contract, originally scheduled to be awarded in September 2018, drew scrutiny after Trump said last year that he was  seriously considering taking a look at the JEDI contract.
 "I never had something where more people are complaining," Trump told reporters at the White House in July 2019. "Some of the greatest companies in the world are complaining about it," he added, naming  IBM, Microsoft and  Oracle. The White House declined to comment.
 Trump's potential influence over the deal  came into focus when a book charting then-Defense Secretary James Mattis' tenure at the Pentagon claimed the president told Mattis to "screw Amazon" out of the contract.
 The billionaire executive, who owns the Washington Post, has become a source of frustration for Trump. The president regularly criticizes the publication for its coverage of his administration. Trump has also gone after Amazon repeatedly for, as he claims,  not paying its fair share of taxes and ripping off the U.S. Post Office.
 Earlier this year, the Pentagon's inspector general released a report saying that the award  did not appear to be influenced by the White House.
 However, the inspector general noted in its 313-page April report that it had limited cooperation from White House officials throughout its review and, as a result, it could not complete its assessment of allegations of ethical misconduct.
  At the time, Department of Defense spokesperson Lt. Col. Robert Carver told CNBC in a statement that the inspector general's report confirmed the Pentagon's JEDI cloud procurement process was conducted "fairly and in accordance with the law."
 An AWS spokesperson suggested at the time that the report did not reveal all that much.
 "It's clear that this report couldn't assess political interference because several DoD witnesses were instructed by the White House not to answer the IG's questions about communications between the White House and DoD officials," the spokesperson wrote in an email to CNBC. "The White House's refusal to cooperate with the IG's investigation is yet another blatant attempt to avoid a meaningful and transparent review of the JEDI contract award."
 In December, Amazon's AWS chief Andy Jassy told CNBC that the cloud contracting was not adjudicated fairly.
 "You know, there was significant political interference here," Jassy said of the JEDI award in a conversation with CNBC's Jon Fortt. "When you have a sitting president who's willing to be very vocal that they dislike a company and the CEO of that company, it makes it difficult for government agencies, including the DoD to make objective decisions without fear of reprisal. And I think that's dangerous and risky for our country."
title: 五角大楼表示将坚持与微软签订100亿美元的绝地云合同
title_en: Pentagon Says It Will Stick With Microsoft For $10 Billion JEDI Cloud Contract

今天，特朗普政府发布了第五份太空政策指令，这份指令旨在为航天行业提出一份关于如何保护航天器免受网络威胁的最佳实践清单。其目标是鼓励政府和航天行业在制定网络安全计划的情况下创建他们的航天器，在设计、制造和操作他们的航天器时纳入加密软件和其他保护等工具。
高级政府官员在背景讲话中声称，目前关注太空网络安全是关键，因为外国对手对太空硬件的威胁越来越大。一位官员表示：“这些威胁是多样而复杂的，加强公私合作以增强基础设施的安全性和弹性是我们努力的关键。”
当被问及哪些具体类型的威胁值得关注时，官员们不愿透露细节。“只要说它们确实发生了，而且它们的发生具有令人担忧的规律性，这样就可以……。一套网络安全原则很重要，“一位官员在背景中表示。这些官员指出，国防部最近发布的一份报告详细描述了过去一年中国国内的军事和安全事态发展。该报告概述了中国不断发展的太空计划，以及中国在发展卫星干扰器和“进攻性网络能力”等武器方面所做的努力。
“只要说它们确实发生了，而且它们的发生具有令人担忧的规律性就够了。”
为了应对这些威胁，空间政策指令5列出了公司在向太空发射卫星和其他飞行器时应该努力遵守的指导方针。管理局建议操作员使用各种类型的软件，以确保他们从航天器接收的数据是加密的。该指令还鼓励公司使用可信的供应链，并监督其地面系统的安全-他们使用这些设施从航天器发送信号和检索数据。报告还建议防止卫星受到干扰和欺骗。一位官员说：“有时候，干扰可能相当粗暴；在其他情况下，如果有人试图登上飞机，一些欺骗可能会相当复杂。”“所以有一系列的东西你需要端到端地看。”
最终，该指令说，政府机构应该与商业公司合作，进一步细化这些最佳网络安全实践应该是什么，特别是考虑到航天行业的许多人在制造和发射飞行器时已经实施了这些战略。一位官员表示：“我认为必须强调的是，我们不是试图强加新的政府驱动的自上而下的要求和标准，而是实际上试图与私营部门合作，并利用它们的杠杆作用。”
SPD-5是特朗普政府旨在塑造美国太空议程的最新政策指令。特朗普的第一个指令指示NASA将人类送回月球，而其他指令则专注于想出一种监督太空交通的方法，并精简太空许可证的监管规定。
note_en:
Today, the Trump administration  released its fifth Space Policy Directive, this one designed to come up with a list of best practices for the space industry on how to protect their spacecraft from cyber threats. The goal is to encourage the government and space industry to create their space vehicles with cybersecurity plans in place, incorporating tools like encryption software and other protections when designing, building, and operating their vehicles.
 Senior administration officials, speaking on background, claimed that focusing on cybersecurity in space is key right now, as there are growing threats to space hardware from foreign adversaries. “These threats are diverse and complex, and robust public-private cooperation to enhance the security and resilience of an infrastructure is key to our efforts,” one official said.
 When asked what specific types of threats were of concern, the officials wouldn’t talk specifics. “Suffice to say that they do occur, and they occur with concerning regularity, such that this... set of cybersecurity principles was important,” an official said on background. The officials pointed to  a recent report from the Defense Department detailing military and security developments within China over the last year. The report outlined China’s growing space program, as well as the country’s efforts to develop weapons like satellite jammers and “offensive cyber capabilities.”
 “Suffice to say that they do occur, and they occur with concerning regularity.”
 To combat these threats, Space Policy Directive 5 lays out guidelines that companies should try to adhere to as they launch satellites and other vehicles to space. The administration is recommending operators use various types of software to ensure that the data they receive from their spacecraft is encrypted. The directive also encourages companies to use trusted supply chains and oversee the safety of their ground systems — the facilities they use to send signals and retrieve data from their spacecraft. The report also recommends protecting against jamming and spoofing of satellites. “Sometimes the jamming can be fairly crude; other cases, some of the spoofing can be fairly sophisticated if somebody’s trying to get on board,” one official said. “So there’s a whole range of things that you need to look at kind of end-to-end.”
 Ultimately, the directive says that government agencies should work with commercial companies to further refine what these best cybersecurity practices should be, especially since many in the space industry already implement these strategies when building and launching vehicles. “I think it’s important to stress that we’re not trying to impose new government-driven top down requirements and standards but are in fact trying to work with and leverage the private sector,” an official said.
 SPD-5 is the latest policy directive from the Trump administration designed to shape the US space agenda. Trump’s first directive instructed NASA to send humans back to the Moon, while other directives have focused on coming up with a way to oversee space traffic and streamlining regulations for space licenses.
title: 特朗普政府发布旨在加强太空网络安全的指令
title_en: Trump Administration Issues Directive Aimed At Enhancing Cybersecurity In Space

零工公司在投票活动中又投入了7000万美元，以保持司机和快递员作为独立承包商的地位-使22日的Yes总资金达到惊人的1.81亿美元，并使其成为加州有史以来票房最高的举措之一。
索诺马州立大学(Sonoma State University)研究加州投票措施的政治学教授大卫·麦昆(David McCuan)说，“选民们最好系好安全带--他们会面对接二连三的广告，并受到支持22的一方的热烈欢迎。”“这是一笔令人难以置信的钱，还有很长的路要走。我们距离选举还有60天。“。
麦昆说，道具。22日可能创下加州倡议历史上支出最高的纪录。他说，1998年授权在部落土地上赌博的第5号提案，按今天的美元计算有3亿美元。气球百科显示了自那时以来最昂贵的措施，从1.21亿美元到1.54亿美元不等(未调整到今天的美元)。
由有组织的劳工资助的22号竞选活动筹集的资金相对较少，仅为480万美元。
根据周五提交给加州国务卿的一份最近提交的捐款表格，优步(Uber)、Lyft、Insta和DoorDash分别在22日向Yes多出资1750万美元。优步(Uber)、Lyft和DoorDash此前各投入了3000万美元。食品杂货送货服务公司Insta之前投资了1000万美元，送货服务Postmate也是如此，优步最近同意收购该公司。
这些公司迫切希望避免不得不将司机和快递员重新归类为员工，他们表示，这会破坏他们的业务所依赖的灵活性。就业也会使他们的成本增加数亿美元。道具。22将永久保持工人作为独立承包商的地位，同时使他们有权获得一些工资保障和一些福利。
除了这笔钱，优步和Lyft还利用了他们庞大的司机和乘客联系信息网络，敦促对Prop的支持。22.。上个月，他们发出了一封电子邮件狂轰滥炸的警告，如果不通过，乘客和司机将面临可怕的后果。
McCuan说，演唱会公司认为这个问题对他们的未来至关重要，所以他们愿意投入这么多钱也就不足为奇了。
他说：“就选票政治的运作方式而言，这是一种付费游戏计划。”
加州根据该州新的零工法律AB5起诉优步和Lyft，称它们错误地对司机进行了分类，剥夺了他们的就业福利和保护。这些公司在法庭上进行了反击，但将主要希望寄托在投票措施上。
No on Prop发言人迈克·罗斯(Mike Roth)表示：“颇具讽刺意味的是，应用程序公司选择了劳动节(周末)，在争取特别豁免法律的活动中再投入7000万美元，以剥夺司机的失业保险和大流行期间带薪病假等福利，”No on Prop的发言人迈克·罗斯(Mike Roth)说。22.。
对22日投赞成票的竞选活动表示，这项措施将保护数十万个就业机会，以及公司提供的服务。优步和Lyft威胁称，如果被迫将司机重新归类为员工，他们将至少暂时离开加州。
“这些都是巨大的赌注，我们将确保全州的每个加州人都能听到我们的信息，即司机不想以4比1的比例成为员工，这是道具。”22号将确保司机的独立性加上历史性的新利益，“是的，22号的发言人杰夫·维特在一份声明中说。
note_en:
Gig companies have poured $70 million more into their ballot campaign to keep drivers and couriers as independent contractors — bringing the total funds for Yes on 22 to a breathtaking $181 million, and putting it on track as one of the biggest-ticket California initiatives ever.
  “Voters better strap in — they’re in for a barrage of ads and sizzle from the pro-22 side,” said David McCuan, a professor of political science at Sonoma State University who studies California ballot measures. “This is an incredible amount of money and there’s a long ways to go. We’re still 60 days out from the election.”
  McCuan said Prop. 22 could set the mark for the most spending in California initiative history. Proposition 5 in 1998, which authorized gambling on tribal lands, had $300 million in today’s dollars, he said.  Ballotpedia shows the most expensive measures since then as ranging from $121 million to $154 million (not adjusted to today’s dollars).
  The No on 22 campaign, which is funded by organized labor, has raised a comparatively modest $4.8 million.
  Uber, Lyft, Instacart and DoorDash each chipped in $17.5 million more to Yes on 22, according to a  Late Contributions form filed with the California Secretary of State on Friday. Uber, Lyft and DoorDash had previously put in $30 million each. Grocery-delivery service Instacart had previously put in $10 million, as had delivery-service Postmates, which  Uber recently agreed to buy.
  The companies urgently want to avoid  having to reclassify their drivers and couriers as employees, which they say would destroy the flexibility their businesses rely on. Employment also would add hundreds of millions of dollars to their costs. Prop. 22 would permanently keep the workers as independent contractors, while entitling them to some wage guarantees and some benefits.
     On top of the money, Uber and Lyft have tapped their vast network of contact information for drivers and riders to urge support for Prop. 22. Last month they sent an email blast warning of dire consequences for riders and drivers if it doesn’t pass.
  The gig companies see the issue as crucial to their future so it’s not surprising that they’re willing to throw so much money at it, McCuan said.
  “It is a pay-to-play scheme in terms of how ballot politics work,” he said.
  California has  sued Uber and Lyft under AB5, the state’s new gig-work law, saying they misclassified drivers and deprived them of the benefits and protections of employment. The companies are fighting back in court, but pinning their main hopes on the ballot measure.
  “Quite an irony the app companies picked Labor Day (weekend) to dump $70 million more into their campaign for a special exemption from the law to deny their drivers benefits like unemployment insurance and paid sick time in a pandemic,” said Mike Roth, spokesman for No on Prop. 22.
  The Yes on 22 campaign said the measure would protect hundreds of thousands of jobs, as well as the services the companies provide. Uber and Lyft have threatened  to leave California, at least temporarily, if they are forced to reclassify drivers as employees.
  “These are enormous stakes and we’ll ensure every Californian across the state is able to hear our message that drivers do not want to be employees by a 4-to-1 margin and that Prop. 22 will ensure driver independence plus historic new benefits,” Yes on 22 spokesman Geoff Vetter said in a statement.
title: 优步和其他公司在加州的投票活动中又增加了7000万美元，以保留零工作为独立承包商，使竞选资金总额达到1.81亿美元
title_en: Uber and others have added $70M more to their California ballot campaign to keep gig workers as independent contractors, bringing total campaign funds to $181M

西雅图初创公司ThruWave刚刚为其使用毫米波透视包装的未来主义技术筹集了640万美元。
财富100强零售和电子商务仓库的客户正在使用该公司的硬件和软件，该公司的硬件和软件最初是由华盛顿大学开发的。
ThruWave的传感器安装在传送带旁边，通过天线和该公司所称的“压缩传感”对里面的物品进行3D成像。计算机视觉软件可以测量立方体利用率，清点物品，并检测丢失或损坏的物品。该系统每小时可以自动成像和分析多达20,000个盒子。
首席执行官马特·雷诺兹(Matt Reynolds)说：“我们都有过在网上订购东西，然后收到一个巨大的浪费的装运箱里的小东西的经历-ThruWave帮助解决了这个问题。”
E14基金管理合伙人Calvin Chin在一份声明中表示：“就整个行业而言，据估计，典型的电子商务货件只有40%满载，导致包装材料、运输成本和碳足迹方面的巨大浪费。”ThruWave公司独特的传感器和软件使客户能够重新控制他们的运输成本和对环境的影响。“。
考虑到随着越来越多的人在网上购买产品，电子商务活动增加，这种流行病正在帮助推动对ThruWave技术的需求。
雷诺兹说：“ThruWave能够实现自动化物料处理的准确性和效率，使我们的客户能够满足这种购物模式的转变，这种转变已经在发生，但随着人们在家购物以帮助减少COVID的传播，这种转变已经急剧加快。”
ThruWave还与美国政府机构的非营利性投资者In-Q-tel合作，将其包装筛查技术用于国家安全应用。雷诺兹说，传感器还可以用来透视墙壁表面，并帮助施工或改建项目。
该公司于2017年从UW剥离出来。雷诺兹于2013年加入威斯康星州大学，担任教授。他之前共同创立了RFID系统公司ThingMagic，该公司于2017年被Trimble导航收购；家庭传感公司SNUPI Technologies，于2015年被西尔斯收购；以及节能初创公司Zensi，于2009年被贝尔金收购。
雷诺兹与威斯康星大学的研究科学家安德烈亚斯·佩德罗斯-恩格尔(Andreas Pedross-Engel)和克莱尔·瓦茨(Claire Watts)共同创立了ThruWave，后者在波士顿学院获得物理学博士学位，并在雷诺兹在威斯康星大学的实验室工作。
这轮投资由E14基金和Ubiquity Ventures牵头，包括Root Ventures、Blue Sky Capital、WRF Capital、清源风险投资公司和In-Q-Tele.。到目前为止，ThruWave已经筹集了860万美元，此外还有来自国家科学基金会的100万美元的小企业创新研究(SBIR)赠款。

note_en:
Seattle startup  ThruWave just raised a $6.4 million round for its futuristic technology that uses millimeter waves to see through packaging.
 Fortune 100 customers in retail and e-commerce warehouses are using the company’s hardware and software, which was first developed at the University of Washington.
 ThruWave’s sensors mount alongside conveyor belts and make 3D images of the items inside via antennas and what the company calls “compressive sensing.” Computer vision software can measure cube utilization, count items, and detect missing or damaged items. The system can automatically image and analyze up to 20,000 boxes per hour.
  “We’ve all had the experience of ordering things online and receiving a tiny little item inside a big wasteful shipping box — ThruWave helps solve that problem,” said CEO  Matt Reynolds.
   “Industry-wide, it is estimated that a typical e-commerce shipment is only 40% full, leading to tremendous waste in packaging material, shipping cost, and carbon footprint,” Calvin Chin, managing partner of E14 Fund, said in a statement. “ThruWave’s unique sensors and software enable customers to regain control over their shipping cost and environmental impact.”
  The pandemic is helping drive demand for ThruWave’s technology given the increased ecommerce activity as more people buy products online.
 “ThruWave enables accuracy and efficiency in automated material handling to enable our customers to meet this shift in shopping patterns, which was already happening but has accelerated dramatically as people shop from home to help reduce the spread of COVID,” Reynolds said.
 ThruWave is also working with In-Q-Tel, a nonprofit investor for U.S. government agencies, to use its packing screening technology for national security applications. Reynolds said the sensors can also be used to see through wall surfaces and help with construction or remodeling projects.
 The company spun out of the UW in 2017. Reynolds joined the UW as a professor in 2013. He previously co-founded RFID systems firm ThingMagic, which was acquired by Trimble Navigation in 2017; home sensing company SNUPI Technologies,  which was acquired by Sears in 2015; and energy conservation startup Zensi, which was acquired by Belkin in 2009.
  Reynolds co-founded ThruWave with  Andreas Pedross-Engel, a research scientist at the UW, and  Claire Watts, who earned her physics Ph. D at Boston College and worked in Reynolds’ lab at the UW.
 The investment round was led by E14 Fund and Ubiquity Ventures, and included participation from Root Ventures, Blue Sky Capital, WRF Capital, Tsingyuan Ventures, and In-Q-Tel. ThruWave has raised $8.6 million to date, in addition to $1 million in Small Business Innovation Research (SBIR) grants from the National Science Foundation.
      GeekWire Space & Science - Weekly headlines from Alan Boyle, award-winning aerospace and science editor
title: ThruWave使用基于毫米波的计算机视觉技术为通过零售仓库的包裹内物品创建3D图像，该公司筹集了640万美元的种子
title_en: ThruWave, which uses millimeter wave-based computer vision tech to create 3D images of items inside packages going through retail warehouses, raises $6.4M seed

note:
华盛顿--今年3月，当特朗普政府下令开展一项研究，允许广泛释放疟疾药物羟氯喹来治疗新冠肺炎时，负责监督这项试验的一家政府研究机构的负责人首先提出的问题之一是：“谁和甲骨文谈过了？”
这家硅谷巨头已经开始准备帮助收集有关该药物的数据，其创始人拉里·埃里森(Larry Ellison)与特朗普总统讨论了它的可能用途。
一些科技公司可能不愿帮助测试一种药物，许多医学专家表示，这种药物具有潜在的危险副作用，甚至可能对新冠肺炎的病例无效。但成立于1977年的商业软件巨头甲骨文(Oracle)是特朗普的重要盟友，特朗普的政府致力于该药物的使用。
甲骨文参与计划中的药物研究是其帮助总统和他的政府的最新努力。该公司还支持政府的贸易计划及其在重大科技政策问题上的立场，其高管在2016年曾在特朗普的过渡团队中发挥作用，并支持他的连任竞选活动。
现在，当它试图收购病毒式社交媒体应用TikTok的美国业务时，它对政府的拥抱可能会有所帮助。特朗普以国家安全担忧为由，命令该应用的中国母公司字节跳动(ByteDance)销售该产品，他的政府必须批准任何交易。
特朗普拒绝透露该公司是否比另一家主要竞购者微软更适合收购这款应用。但他上个月表示，甲骨文“肯定会是能处理这件事的人”。最近几天，在中国政府发布了似乎使出售变得更加复杂的新规定后，谈判遇到了障碍。
在杜克大学(Duke University)任教的前Facebook政策主管马特·佩罗(Matt Perault)说，与许多最大的科技公司不同，甲骨文有一种商业模式--向企业和政府销售软件和服务--这让它能够在不危及自己品牌的情况下与特朗普发展关系。
“甲骨文可以与总统保持密切关系，而不会疏远客户基础，”佩罗说，“这是像Twitter这样的公司所没有的奢侈品。”
甲骨文发言人黛博拉·海林格(Deborah Hellinger)拒绝置评。白宫也拒绝置评。
甲骨文一直在与TikTok的一些美国投资者合作，比如私募股权公司通用大西洋(General Atlantic)和风险投资公司红杉资本(Sequoia Capital)。另一个竞标集团包括微软(Microsoft)和沃尔玛(Walmart)。
围绕可能的出售，已经有一场激烈的企业游说战。微软总裁布拉德·史密斯(Brad Smith)上个月前往华盛顿，与白宫官员和国会山的议员们讨论了如果微软购买这款应用，该公司将如何缓解人们的担忧。甲骨文宣布，它将加入国务院的清洁网络计划，该计划旨在对抗中国对全球技术的影响。
根据响应性政治中心(Center For Responsive Politics)的数据，甲骨文去年在联邦游说上花费了821万美元，雇佣了59名游说者。相比之下，谷歌的支出为1,278万美元，微软为1,030万美元。甲骨文华盛顿办事处由肯·格鲁克(Ken Glueck)执掌，他在20世纪90年代曾是康涅狄格州民主党参议员约瑟夫·I·利伯曼(Joseph I.Lieberman)的助手。
它利用这种影响行动，以不情愿的竞争强度追逐少数竞争对手。多年来，它一直与谷歌就一个晦涩的版权问题陷入诉讼。它不仅在此案上攻击了该公司，还用反垄断问题困扰着它，并就它所说的谷歌滥用消费者数据进行了技术研究。甲骨文还与亚马逊争夺一份价值100亿美元的军事合同，并指责这家零售商试图操纵这一过程。
不过，它与白宫的密切联系才是它在硅谷脱颖而出的真正原因。
一位熟悉甲骨文做法的人士表示，虽然许多公司对特朗普在2016年的胜利感到措手不及，但甲骨文在选举日前不久就开始在他的政治圈子里建立关系。由于讨论是私下进行的，该人士不愿具名。甲骨文首席执行官萨夫拉·卡茨(Safra Catz)是唯一加入特朗普政府过渡团队执行委员会的主要科技高管，该团队在新政府上台前准备政策和计划。(风险投资家、Facebook董事会成员彼得·泰尔(Peter Thiel)也是该委员会的成员。)。格鲁埃克还加入了更广泛的过渡团队。
这位熟悉甲骨文做法的人士表示，甲骨文的代表与即将上任的政府在税收、贸易和政府合同相关的问题上进行了合作，这些都是该公司的主要业务来源。
该公司还雇佣了与特朗普有联系的人。它的外部说客之一大卫·厄本(David Urban)是国务卿迈克·庞皮欧(Mike Pompeo)的西点军校同学。另一个是马特·施拉普(Matt Schlapp)，他的妻子梅赛德斯·施拉普(Mercedes Schlapp)是特朗普连任竞选活动的高级顾问，也是前白宫通讯官员。
它与白宫的关系引起了公司内外的一些关注。劳工部的一名举报人上个月表示，劳工部长尤金·斯卡利亚(Eugene Scalia)干预了针对该公司的歧视诉讼。该部门表示，斯卡利亚没有任何不当行为，甲骨文称此案毫无根据。
当甲骨文创始人兼董事长埃里森今年在南加州的一个家中为特朗普举办筹款活动时，一些员工对此表示抗议。
尽管受到批评，该公司仍在坚持不懈。联邦记录显示，卡茨已经捐赠了超过13万美元来支持特朗普连任。甲骨文的另一位高管杰弗里·亨利(Jeffrey Henley)已经捐赠了超过5.5万美元。去年，该公司向一个由共和党特工建立的团体捐赠了50万至999,999美元，目的是推动特朗普的北美贸易协定。
“纽约时报”获得的文件显示，今年3月，当生物医学高级研究与发展局(Biomedical Advanced Research And Development Authority)局长里克·布莱特(Rick Bright)询问同事关于启动羟氯喹研究的命令时，他说，该项目的细节“非常粗略，指令是迅速采取行动”。该研究将由他的机构监督。
他很快就收到了美国食品和药物管理局(Food And Drug Administration)首席律师斯泰西·阿明(Stacy Amin)的回复。阿明女士说，总统“今晚宣布这件事，我相信世界卫生组织希望明天能把它建立起来，让数据流入甲骨文平台。”
布莱特最终在4月份被免职，他说这是因为他对羟基氯喹的疑虑造成的。他已经提交了一份正式的举报人投诉。这项研究和公告从未取得成果。(政府高级官员回击了他的说法，称他的指控站不住脚。)。
但在他就将这些药物带给患者的计划进行初步交流不到一个月后，该部门宣布，甲骨文已经捐赠了一个平台，用于治疗新冠肺炎，以“从医生和其他临床医生那里收集关于患者对可能的治疗方法的反应的众包实时信息”。
他说：“甲骨文正在与H.H.S.和特朗普政府合作，提供数据驱动的解决方案，这是特朗普总统领导的打击新冠肺炎的全美方式的又一个切实成果。”
note_en:
WASHINGTON — In March, when the Trump administration ordered up a study enabling the widespread release of the malaria drug  hydroxychloroquine to treat Covid-19, one of the first questions the director of a government research agency that would oversee the trial asked was: “Who has talked with Oracle?”
 The Silicon Valley powerhouse had already started to prepare to help with collecting data about the drug, and its founder, Larry Ellison, talked to President Trump about its possible use.
 Some tech companies may have shied away from helping to test a drug that many medical experts said had potentially dangerous side effects and might not even work for Covid-19 cases. But Oracle, a business software giant founded in 1977, is a prominent ally of Mr. Trump, whose administration was invested in the drug’s use.
 Oracle’s involvement in the planned drug study was its latest effort to aid the president and his administration. The company has also backed the administration’s trade plans and its positions on major tech policy issues, and its executives played roles in Mr. Trump’s transition team in 2016 and have backed his re-election campaign.
 Now, as it tries to buy the  U.S. operations of TikTok, the viral social media app, its embrace of the administration could be helpful.  Mr. Trump ordered the app’s Chinese parent company, ByteDance, to sell the product, citing national security concerns, and his administration must bless any deal.
  Mr. Trump has declined to say whether the company is a better suitor for the app than Microsoft, another major bidder. But he said last month that Oracle “would be certainly somebody that could handle it.”  The negotiations hit a snag in recent days, after the Chinese government issued new rules that seem to make a sale more complicated.
 Matt Perault, a former policy executive at Facebook who teaches at Duke University, said that unlike many of the biggest tech companies, Oracle had a business model — selling software and services to businesses and governments — that allowed it to develop a relationship with Mr. Trump without putting its brand at risk.
 “Oracle can be close to the president without alienating the customer base,” Mr. Perault said, “and that’s a luxury that a company like Twitter doesn’t have.”
 A spokeswoman for Oracle, Deborah Hellinger, declined to comment. The White House also declined to comment.
 Oracle has pursued TikTok with  some of the app’s American investors, like the private equity firm General Atlantic and the venture capital firm Sequoia Capital. The  other bidding group includes Microsoft and Walmart.
 There is already a pitched corporate lobbying battle over the possible sale. Brad Smith, Microsoft’s president, traveled to Washington last month to talk with White House officials and lawmakers on Capitol Hill about how the company would assuage concerns if it bought the app. Oracle announced that it was joining the State Department’s Clean Network program, which is aimed at combating China’s influence over global technology.
 Oracle spent $8.21 million on federal lobbying and employed 59 lobbyists last year, according to the Center for Responsive Politics. In comparison, Google spent $12.78 million and Microsoft $10.3 million. Oracle’s Washington office is run by Ken Glueck, who in the 1990s was an aide to Senator Joseph I. Lieberman, a Democrat from Connecticut.
 It has used that influence operation to pursue a small number of rivals with grudge-match intensity. For years, it has been locked in a lawsuit with Google over an obscure copyright question. It has not just attacked the company over the case, but has dogged it with antitrust questions and produced technical research on what it says are Google’s abuses of consumer data. Oracle also competed with Amazon for a $10 billion military contract and accused the retailer of trying to rig the process.
 Its close ties with the White House, though, are what really make it stand out in Silicon Valley.
  While many companies were caught off guard by Mr. Trump’s victory in 2016, Oracle had begun to build relationships in his political universe shortly before Election Day, said one person familiar with its approach. The person would speak only on the condition of anonymity because the discussions were private. Safra Catz, Oracle’s chief executive, was the only major tech executive to join the executive committee of the Trump administration’s transition team, the group that prepares policies and plans before a new administration takes office. (Peter Thiel, a venture capitalist and Facebook board member, also served on the committee.) Mr. Glueck also joined the broader transition team.
 Oracle’s representatives worked with the incoming administration on issues related to taxes, trade and government contracts, which are a major source of business for the company, said the person familiar with its approach.
 The company has also hired people with ties to Mr. Trump. One of its outside lobbyists, David Urban, was a West Point classmate of Secretary of State Mike Pompeo. Another is Matt Schlapp, whose wife, Mercedes Schlapp, is a senior adviser to Mr. Trump’s re-election campaign and a former White House communications official.
 Its relationship with the White House has attracted some concern from inside and outside the company. A  whistle-blower at the Labor Department said last month that Eugene Scalia, the secretary of labor, had interfered in a discrimination lawsuit against the company. The department said Mr. Scalia had done nothing untoward, and Oracle said the case was groundless.
 When Mr. Ellison, Oracle’s founder and chairman, hosted a fund-raiser for Mr. Trump this year at a home in Southern California, some employees protested the move.
 The company has pressed on despite the criticism. Ms. Catz has donated more than $130,000 to support Mr. Trump’s re-election, federal records show. Another Oracle executive, Jeffrey Henley, has donated more than $55,000. Last year,  the company gave $500,000 to $999,999 to a group established by Republican operatives to push for Mr. Trump’s North American trade deal.
  In March, when  Rick Bright, the director of the Biomedical Advanced Research and Development Authority, asked colleagues about orders to start a study of hydroxychloroquine that his agency would oversee, he said details on the project were “very sketchy, and the directive is to move quickly,” according to documents obtained by The New York Times.
 He soon received a reply from Stacy Amin, the top lawyer at the Food and Drug Administration. The president, Ms. Amin said, “is announcing this tonight, and I believe the WH would like it set up by tomorrow with data to flow into the Oracle platform.”
 Mr. Bright was ultimately  removed from his role in April, which he says was the result of his doubts over hydroxychloroquine. He has filed  a formal whistle-blower complaint. The study and the announcement never came to fruition. (Senior administration officials have pushed back on his account, saying his allegations don’t hold up.)
 But a little less than a month after his initial exchanges over the plans to bring the drugs to patients, the department announced that Oracle had donated a platform to “gather crowdsourced, real-time information from doctors and other clinicians about how patients are responding to possible therapeutics” used to treat Covid-19.
  “The work Oracle is doing with H.H.S. and the Trump administration to deliver data-driven solutions is another tangible result of the all-of-America approach President Trump has led to combat Covid-19,” he said.
title: 在甲骨文试图收购TikTok之际，看看该公司在2016年大选后如何开始拥抱唐纳德·特朗普(Donald Trump)和他的政府，并与他们建立关系
title_en: As Oracle tries to buy TikTok, a look at how the company started embracing and building ties with Donald Trump and his administration after the 2016 election
